task_completion.description: Verify the location and time are correctly set in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium bottom toolbar, Location window, or Date/Time window showing the location as 30° N, 40° W (or very close) and the date/time as 2024-01-15 22:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Verify the requested display settings are applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky view must show constellation lines, a circular azimuthal coordinate grid, cardinal direction letters (N, S, E, W), and the ground/landscape must be disabled (stars should be visible below the horizon line).

task_completion.description: Verify Sirius is located and centered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the star Sirius selected (typically indicated by a target reticle and information text in the top left corner) and centered in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify Canopus is located and centered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the star Canopus selected and centered in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify Polaris is located and centered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the star Polaris selected and centered in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify Jupiter is located and centered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the planet Jupiter selected and centered in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify screenshots were saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows at least 4 image files located in the /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the navigation star log file is created with the correct details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/nav_star_log.txt containing the names of the four targets (Sirius, Canopus, Polaris, Jupiter), the observation date (Jan 15, 2024), and the observer position (30N 40W).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium application interface (toolbars, menus, or rendering engine) rather than a web browser searching for images of stars.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshots match the configured environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshots of the stars/planets must exhibit the specific display configurations requested (e.g., no ground, azimuthal grid visible, constellation lines), proving they were taken from the configured Stellarium session and not downloaded.

integrity.description: Verify the log file was genuinely created in the environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The log file should be visible in a standard text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) or via a terminal command (like cat), rather than just being a pasted text block in a terminal without file creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Targets to verify: Sirius, Canopus, Polaris, Jupiter. Expected location: 30.00° N, 40.00° W (0.5236 rad, -0.6981 rad). Expected date/time: January 15, 2024, 22:00 UTC. Required display settings: Atmosphere ON, Constellation lines ON, Azimuthal grid ON, Cardinal points ON, Ground/landscape OFF. Log file path: /home/ga/Desktop/nav_star_log.txt. Screenshots path: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium (minimum 4 screenshots).
